mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 8.5 percent, labor market slack dissipates sufficiently to trigger wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy momentum. The joint rise in supply capacity and demand absorption confirms a healthy business cycle, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least 60% of rolling 12-month periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, accelerating real GDP growth drives equity valuations higher. The mechanism relies on the demand recovery phase where corporate profit margins expand alongside top-line growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, indicating the economy has transitioned to late-cycle overheating rather than recovery, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization improvements. Higher output levels reduce input bottlenecks and support near-term corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 3-month Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 225, inflation expectations anchor firmly, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy longer than markets anticipate. This delayed tightening cycle supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are significantly negative when CPIAUCSL crosses above 225, indicating the market prices in imminent aggressive tightening, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms that labor market improvements are translating into actual consumer spending. This demand-supply validation loop reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k monthly but RSAFS growth stagnates, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero lower bound environment, further reductions in the federal funds rate have diminishing marginal impact on discount rates but signal persistent accommodative liquidity. This liquidity dominance effect compresses risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises above 230 while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, indicating inflation fears override liquidity effects, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 2-year test period.
